improve this codebase, focusing on cleaning up superfluous 'features', improving performance, and fixing bugs. Clean up dead code, code smells, dead tests, and tautological tests. ## Your scouting topic (stay focused here — other scouts are covering the rest)
Topic: Backend .NET Web API (production)
Rationale: This is the server-side core and the only C# production code, so it is distinct from all other topics. It carries several concrete targets the task names. Duplicate code: FilesController.cs repeats an identical 5-catch block (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException) across all six endpoints (~30 catches); the last two are IOException subtypes, so those catches are redundant ordering. Dead code: the injected `_logger` field is stored but never referenced. Performance: `new FileExtensionContentTypeProvider()` is instantiated on every Download request instead of cached; Browse does two directory enumerations (GetDirectories + GetFiles) where EnumerateFileSystemEntries would suffice. Bugs/hardening: Move/Copy use `Path.GetDirectoryName(destination)!` null-forgiving (throws when destination has no parent); the singleton FileService does check-then-act (Directory.Exists then Delete) TOCTOU; Upload returns a path built from the raw, unnormalized `path` query parameter. Files: Controllers/FilesController.cs, Services/FileService.cs, Services/IFileService.cs, Models/FileSystemModels.cs, Program.cs, FileServiceOptions.cs, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json, TestProject.csproj
Key files to start from: Controllers/FilesController.cs, Services/FileService.cs, Services/IFileService.cs, Models/FileSystemModels.cs, Program.cs, FileServiceOptions.cs, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json, TestProject.csproj

Provide a detailed report of your findings. For each finding, briefly note how it is relevant to the overall task. Do not duplicate work that falls outside your topic.

You are scouting ONE focused area of a codebase to support a larger task. Investigate YOUR assigned topic specifically, and relate everything you find back to why it matters for the task below.

## Overall task (the goal this scouting supports)
improve this codebase, focusing on cleaning up superfluous 'features', improving performance, and fixing bugs. Clean up dead code, code smells, dead tests, and tautological tests. Break up monolith files into domain-specific modules. Harden against runtime issues like memory leaks and race conditions, and deadlocks. Refactor at will, this is not a production product. Remember to keep the code interesting and highlight the interesting parts of the codebase. The original prompt is here: task.md. Ensure that the result remains true to it.

## Your scouting topic (stay focused here — other scouts are covering the rest)
Topic: Frontend SPA production code (TypeScript + CSS)
Rationale: The client-side app and the only owner of src/*.ts (non-test) and the stylesheet, so it does not overlap the test topics. Monolith: app.ts (472 lines) mixes DOM construction, event wiring, route dispatch, and every table/row/breadcrumb builder — the task explicitly asks to break monoliths into domain modules (e.g. dom-builders, render, toolbar-handlers). Dead code: parseHash, parentPath, and basename are imported into app.ts but never used; renderBrowse/renderSearch accept a `route` parameter they discard via `void route`. Memory leak: `subscribe(render)` throws away the returned unsubscribe function, so the hashchange listener and module-level DOM refs accumulate across remounts. Dead/mismatched CSS: `.folder-row`, `.breadcrumb .separator`, and the entire `.toolbar label.upload-btn` block target classes the TS never emits (the upload label uses `btn`, the separator span has no class). Magic values: the literal 1024 in formatBytes. Files: src/app.ts, src/api.ts, src/router.ts, src/format.ts, src/app.css, wwwroot/index.html
Key files to start from: src/app.ts, src/api.ts, src/router.ts, src/format.ts, src/app.css, wwwroot/index.html

Provide a detailed report of your findings. For each finding, briefly note how it is relevant to the overall task. Do not duplicate work that falls outside your topic.

tasks.title: Fix TOCTOU race in FileService.Delete
tasks.prompt: In `Services/FileService.cs`, the `Delete` method has a check-then-act race: `if (Directory.Exists(path)) { Directory.Delete(path, recursive: true); } else { File.Delete(path); }`. Between the `Exists` check and the delete call, another process or thread could remove the entry, causing `Directory.Delete` to throw `DirectoryNotFoundException` (or, in the other branch, `File.Delete` silently no-ops which is already safe). Replace with a race-free pattern. Recommended approach: attempt `Directory.Delete(path, recursive: true)`; if it throws `DirectoryNotFoundException`, fall through to `File.Delete(path)` (which is itself a no-op if the file is already gone); wrap both in a single try/catch that treats the 'already gone' outcome as success (since the post-condition — the entry no longer exists — is satisfied). Document the chosen behavior in the docstring (e.g., 'Deleting a non-existent path succeeds silently — the post-condition (entry gone) is already met'). Do NOT change the `SafeResolve(relativePath)` path-safety check that runs first and still throws `ArgumentException` for escape attempts. The lazy `Root` accessor added by task-16 must be used in place of the previous `_root` field. tasks.title: Fix frontend rendering lifecycle (hashchange listener leak + render race)
tasks.prompt:
In `src/app.ts`, two related lifecycle bugs in the rendering system must be fixed together (they share the startApp/render code path):

(a) MEMORY LEAK — `startApp` calls `subscribe(render)` and DISCARDS the returned unsubscribe function. Every call to `startApp` (e.g., across re-mounts, hot reloads, or repeated test setup) accumulates a new `hashchange` listener on `window` that is never removed. The bug is documented in a code comment in `src/app.test.ts`. Fix: introduce a module-level variable `let unsubscribe: (() => void) | null = null;` alongside the existing module-level DOM refs (`resultsEl`, etc.). At the top of `startApp`, if `unsubscribe` is non-null, call it (to remove the previous listener) and set it back to null. Then assign `unsubscribe = subscribe(render);`. This guarantees at most one active listener per mount.

(b) RENDER RACE — `render()` is async. If the user navigates twice in quick succession (e.g., clicks a folder then clicks a sub-folder before the first fetch resolves), two `render()` calls race and the slower one's stale data can overwrite the faster one's fresh data in `resultsEl`. Fix: introduce a module-level monotonic counter `let renderToken = 0;`. At the start of `render()`, increment the counter and capture `const myToken = ++renderToken;`. After EACH `await` inside `render()`, check `if (myToken !== renderToken) return;` — if a newer render has started, abort WITHOUT touching `resultsEl`, `breadcrumbEl`, or `statusEl`. Only the most-recently-started render gets to commit its data (i.e., reach the `renderBrowse(...)` / `renderSearch(...)` calls).

These changes are interdependent (both touch startApp/render state) so they belong in one task. Preserve all currently-observable behavior — the existing `src/app.test.ts` tests must still pass. In particular, the existing comment in app.test.ts's afterEach notes that hashchange listeners accumulate and so the test resets the hash before unstubbing fetch; that comment may become outdated after this fix and should be updated (by the test-writing phase). Verification: `npm test` passes; the test-writing phase may add new tests for (i) multiple `startApp` calls do not multiply hashchange listeners, and (ii) a slower fetch that resolves after a newer navigation is discarded (its data does not appear in `.results`).

result.feedback: Both lifecycle bugs are fixed exactly as specified. (a) The unsubscribe teardown is stored module-level, cleared-and-recalled on every startApp re-mount, guaranteeing at most one active hashchange listener per mount. (b) A monotonic renderToken is captured per render() invocation, and a staleness check after every await (browse fetch, search fetch, and in the catch block) bails out before touching resultsEl/breadcrumbEl/statusEl, so a slower superseded fetch can never clobber fresh results. The changes are minimally scoped, follow the existing comment/variable-placement conventions, and are well documented. The outdated afterEach comment in app.test.ts was correctly updated, and three new lifecycle tests (listener cleanup adds/removes, race discard for results, race discard for breadcrumb/status, plus a non-race control case) pin the new contract. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with no errors and `npm test` passes all 246 tests. No issues found.

tasks.title: Wrap action button click handlers with error handling
tasks.prompt: In `src/app.ts`, the `makeActionButton(label, handler)` helper attaches a click handler that may be async (the Delete/Move/Copy handlers all do `await api.X(...)`). If the API call rejects (e.g., the server returns 400 Bad Request for an invalid destination, or 500 for a server error), the rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection — silently swallowed or surfaced only in the browser console, with no feedback to the user. Fix: in `makeActionButton`, wrap the handler invocation so that if it throws or rejects, the error is surfaced to the user. Recommended approach: the wrapper calls `Promise.resolve(handler()).catch(err => { statusEl.textContent = 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })` (or similar). The handler may now be async without leaking a rejection. Do NOT change the success-path behavior — when the handler resolves normally, no error message is shown. (Note: the status-footer error display is consistent with how `render()` already surfaces fetch errors, so this gives the user a single, consistent error surface.) Verification: `npm test` passes; the test-writing phase may add a test where a Delete/Move/Copy API call rejects and assert the error appears in `.status` (or whatever surface the implementer chose).

result.feedback: Implementation is complete, correct, and well-tested. `makeActionButton` wraps the handler so both synchronous throws (outer try/catch — necessary because `handler()` is evaluated as an argument to `Promise.resolve` before the promise is created, so a bare `.catch` would miss a sync throw) and async rejections (`.catch`) surface errors to the user via the `.status` footer. The error format exactly matches `render()`'s catch block, giving a single consistent error surface as intended. The success path is provably unchanged: tests assert no 'Error' text appears on success or cancel, and the handler still calls `render()` on success. A clear docstring documents the wrapping contract. Comprehensive new tests cover rejecting Delete/Move/Copy (500/400/409), a non-Error bare-string rejection (exercising the `String(err)` fallback), the results-table-left-intact invariant, unchanged success paths, and cancel paths. All 250 tests pass. Only `makeActionButton` was touched — scope is correctly contained. One minor DRY observation: the error-formatting expression is now duplicated in three places (render() catch + the two branches of makeActionButton's wrapper); extracting a tiny `showError(err)` helper would consolidate it, but this is non-blocking since the task explicitly recommended the inline form.

tasks.title: Handle per-file errors in the upload loop
tasks.prompt: In `src/app.ts`, the `handleUpload` function loops over the selected files calling `await api.upload(path, file)` sequentially. If any single upload fails (network error, 413 too large, 400 invalid name), the entire loop aborts via unhandled rejection, the remaining uploads are silently skipped, the input is not cleared, and the user gets no feedback. Fix: wrap each per-file `api.upload` call in its own try/catch (or use `Promise.allSettled` if you convert to parallel — but sequential is fine and preserves ordering). Track the failed file names in a local array. After the loop, regardless of partial failures: (1) clear `uploadInput.value` (so the same file can be re-selected); (2) call `render()` to refresh the listing; (3) if any uploads failed, surface them via the status footer (e.g., `statusEl.textContent = 'Uploaded N file(s); failed: a.txt, b.txt'`). If all succeeded, leave the success-path status alone (the re-render will refresh it via renderBrowse/renderSearch). Do NOT change the upload endpoint, the input clearing, or the re-render on full success. Verification: `npm test` passes (the existing 'uploads each selected file' test still passes); the test-writing phase may add a test where one of several uploads fails (mock the first to resolve and the second to reject) and assert the remaining file is still uploaded, the input is cleared, and the failure is surfaced.

After the loop, uploadInput.value is always cleared, render() is awaited to refresh the listing, and failures are surfaced with the exact requested message format `Uploaded N file(s); failed: a.txt, b.txt` (where N = files.length - failed.length). The full-success path is correctly left alone via the `if (failed.length > 0)` guard, so the re-render's normal status summary persists. The upload endpoint, input clearing, and re-render on success are unchanged. Comments clearly explain the deliberate ordering (render before surfacing failure so the message isn't overwritten). tasks.title: Extract the duplicated 5-catch block into a controller helper
tasks.prompt:
In `Controllers/FilesController.cs`, each of the seven endpoints (Browse, Search, Upload, Download, Delete, Move, Copy) wraps its work in an IDENTICAL five-catch block (`ArgumentException`, `UnauthorizedAccessException`, `DirectoryNotFoundException`, `FileNotFoundException`, `IOException` — each translated to `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })`). That is roughly 210 lines of duplicated boilerplate. Extract a private helper that wraps a delegate and performs the translation, so each endpoint becomes a one-line delegation:

  - `private IActionResult Execute(Func<IActionResult> body)` — synchronous endpoints. Body: `try { return body(); } catch (ArgumentException ex) { return BadRequest(...); } catch (UnauthorizedAccessException ex) { ... } catch (DirectoryNotFoundException ex) { ... } catch (FileNotFoundException ex) { ... } catch (IOException ex) { ... }`
  - `private async Task<IActionResult> ExecuteAsync(Func<Task<IActionResult>> body)` — for the Upload endpoint (which must await the service call). Same catch list.

Then refactor each endpoint to delegate:
  - Browse → `Execute(() => Ok(_service.Browse(path ?? string.Empty)))`
  - Search → `Execute(() => Ok(_service.Search(query ?? string.Empty, path ?? string.Empty)))`
  - Upload → `ExecuteAsync(async () => { await _service.UploadAsync(path ?? string.Empty, file); return Ok(new { path = /* existing normalized-path logic from task-15 */ }); })`
  - Download → `Execute(() => { var full = _service.ResolveFullPath(path); var contentType = ContentTypeProvider.TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream"; return PhysicalFile(full, contentType, Path.GetFileName(full)); })`
  - Delete → `Execute(() => { _service.Delete(path); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`
  - Move → `Execute(() => { _service.Move(request); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`
  - Copy → `Execute(() => { _service.Copy(request); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`

PRESERVE EVERY OBSERVABLE BEHAVIOR — the response status codes (200/400), the `error`/`success`/`path` payload shapes, the exact exception-to-BadRequest mapping, and especially the 'non-translated exceptions propagate' guarantee covered by `Controller_DoesNotSwallowUnhandledExceptions` in FilesControllerTests.cs. While some tests exist, they have significant gaps that could allow the refactored code to fail without detection. The test suite must be enhanced before this refactoring can be considered safe.

result.feedback: The implementation is complete, correct, and well-documented. Both Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) helpers were extracted with the identical 5-catch translation list, and all seven endpoints (Browse, Search, Upload, Download, Delete, Move, Copy) delegate to them as one-liners while preserving their XML doc comments. The build succeeds with 0 warnings/errors and all 287 tests pass without modification, including the per-endpoint unhandled-exception-propagation tests, subtype-exception cases (ArgumentNullException, ArgumentOutOfRangeException, PathTooLongException), exact-payload-shape assertions, and single-invocation-counting tests. tasks.title: Split app.ts monolith into focused domain modules
tasks.prompt:
`src/app.ts` is a ~470-line monolith that mixes DOM construction, route dispatch, toolbar handlers, browse rendering, search rendering, and DOM builder helpers — six distinct responsibilities. Split it into focused modules under a new `src/app/` directory, each with a single responsibility:

  - `src/app/dom-builders.ts` — exports `buildTable`, `makeNavLink`, `makeParentRow`, `makeBrowseRow`, `makeActionButton`, `makeSearchRow`, `renderBreadcrumb`. These are pure DOM-construction helpers; they need imports of `api` (for download URLs in makeBrowseRow/makeSearchRow), `formatBytes`/`formatDate` (for cell content), and `toBrowseHash`/`navigate` (for nav links). They also need the action-button handler wiring (which now calls back into the render orchestrator — see below) so accept an `onRerender` callback or import the orchestrator lazily.
  - `src/app/render-browse.ts` — exports `renderBrowse`. Operates against the shared DOM context (passed in or via a context module — see below).
  - `src/app/render-search.ts` — exports `renderSearch`.
  - `src/app/toolbar-handlers.ts` — exports `doSearch`, `handleUpload`.
  - `src/app/render-orchestrator.ts` — exports `render` (the route dispatcher), owns the module-level DOM refs (`resultsEl`, `statusEl`, `breadcrumbEl`, `searchInput`, `uploadInput`), the render token (task-20), the unsubscribe handle (task-20), and an `init(domRefs)` or similar that startApp calls to bind the refs.
  - `src/app.ts` (slim entry) — exports `startApp` (constructs the DOM via dom-builders, wires events, binds refs into the orchestrator, kicks off the initial render) and the bootstrap guard (`const appRoot = document.getElementById('app'); if (appRoot) startApp(appRoot);`). RE-EXPORT `renderBrowse` and `renderSearch` from `./app/render-browse.js` and `./app/render-search.js` so `src/app.test.ts`'s existing `import { startApp, renderBrowse, renderSearch } from './app'` continues to resolve without modification.

A clean pattern for the shared DOM context: define a small `src/app/context.ts` (or put it in render-orchestrator.ts) that holds the refs and exposes getters/setters. dom-builders and the render functions read from this context. This replaces the current pattern of bare module-level `let resultsEl!: HTMLElement` variables that live in app.ts.

PRESERVE ALL OBSERVABLE BEHAVIOR — the public API of `app.ts` is unchanged (same exports, same signatures, same DOM output, same event handling, same navigation, same error surfaces). This is a PURELY STRUCTURAL refactor; the test-writing phase should produce characterization tests first (pinning current behavior) so the change is provably behavior-preserving. Verify: `npm run build` succeeds; `npm test` passes (all existing `src/app.test.ts` scenarios still pass without modification).

result.feedback:
The structural refactor is complete, faithful, and behavior-preserving. All seven planned modules exist under src/app/ with exactly the exports the task specified: dom-builders.ts (buildTable, makeNavLink, makeParentRow, makeBrowseRow, makeActionButton, makeSearchRow, renderBreadcrumb), render-browse.ts (renderBrowse), render-search.ts (renderSearch), toolbar-handlers.ts (doSearch, handleUpload), render-orchestrator.ts (render + init(refs) owning renderToken/unsubscribe), and context.ts holding the shared refs/getters/setters (an option the task explicitly permitted). app.ts is now a slim entry that exports startApp, re-exports renderBrowse/renderSearch, and keeps the bootstrap guard — so src/app.test.ts's existing `import { startApp, renderBrowse, renderSearch } from './app'` resolves unmodified.

Behavior preservation verified by line-by-line comparison against git HEAD:src/app.ts. Every helper maps 1:1 with only the indirection swaps the context pattern requires (resultsEl→getResults(), api→getApi(), statusEl→getStatus(), render()→rerender()). The render-hook indirection is sound: init() calls setRenderHook(render) BEFORE the initial render(), so action-button handlers (created only during render) always invoke the real render, and the default no-op hook is never reachable from a real click. The render-race token and listener-teardown logic moved intact into render-orchestrator.ts, preserving the task-20 fixes. The chosen context+hook pattern cleanly breaks the would-be cycle (orchestrator→render-browse→dom-builders→orchestrator) and is well documented in context.ts — a better solution than the 'lazy import' the task mentioned as an alternative.

Verification results: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0 (clean type-check). `npm test` passes all 301 tests, including the 82 unmodified app.test.ts scenarios and 40 new characterization tests (src/app.characterization.test.ts) that pin exact DOM/attribute/lifecycle details. No unused imports/variables, no dead code, no debug statements, no leftover TODOs, and all new public functions carry docstrings. Code follows existing conventions (textContent/element creation for user strings, setAttribute for hrefs, .js import specifiers).

One environment note (NOT a code defect): `npm run build` fails at the copy-assets.mjs step because node_modules/bootstrap-icons/font/bootstrap-icons.css is absent in this worktree — a stale/incomplete node_modules state unrelated to the refactor. The TypeScript compilation half (`tsc`) succeeds; the failure is on a static-asset copy that has no connection to these changes and would fail identically on the base branch. No action needed in code.

tasks.title: Split app.test.ts monolith into per-domain test files
tasks.prompt:
`src/app.test.ts` is a ~940-line monolith covering six distinct concerns in one file. Split it into focused test files matching the production-module decomposition from task-28:

  - `src/app/start-app.test.ts` — the 'app module surface' and 'startApp' describe blocks (module exports, DOM structure, dialog open/close wiring, shared-widget embedded-vs-dialog behavior).
  - `src/app/render-browse.test.ts` — the entire `renderBrowse` describe block (table headers, rows, columns, navigation, actions column, breadcrumb, status footer).
  - `src/app/render-search.test.ts` — the entire `renderSearch` describe block (table headers, rows, navigation, status).
  - `src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts` — the 'render orchestration' describe block (initial render on startApp, dispatch after navigation, error display, clear-previous-results).
  - `src/app/toolbar-handlers.test.ts` — the 'toolbar handlers' describe block (search button, Enter key, upload happy path and empty-files case).

The shared test helpers (`mockResponse`, `fileEntry`, `browseResult`, `joinPathHelper`, `browseRoute`, `searchRoute`, `flush`, `SetupCtx`, `setup`, `setupCleared`, `dataRows`, `rowByName`, `cellsOf`, `clickNameLink`, `buttonsByText`, and the test lifecycle `beforeEach`/`afterEach` that stubs `fetch`) must be extracted into a shared helper module such as `src/app/test-helpers.ts` (and excluded from the production build via the tsconfig `exclude` array, similar to task-27). Each split test file imports what it needs. If `mockResponse` was already extracted to `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts` by task-27, import from there instead of re-defining. PRESERVE EVERY EXISTING TEST SCENARIO — do not drop, weaken, or merge assertions. The total test count after the split must equal the total before (minus anything task-7 already removed). Verify: `npm test` passes; the distributed test files together cover the same surface as the original monolith.

result.feedback: The split is a clean, behavior-preserving refactor. VERIFIED COMPLETION: (1) All 89 `it()` test scenarios are preserved exactly — I extracted every test title from the original monolith and from the 5 split files and confirmed a perfect multiset match (0 dropped, 0 added, 0 renamed). I also diffed the full body of every top-level describe block (whitespace-normalized); the ONLY textual difference in any assertion/comment is `'./app'` → `'../app'` in one comment, which is the correct path adjustment for the new `src/app/` location. (2) `npm test` passes: 333 tests across 12 files, including 89 in the 5 new split files. For comparison, I stashed the changes and re-ran the ORIGINAL monolith — it had 5 FAILURES (`ReferenceError: browseRoute is not defined` / `searchRoute is not defined` in the dead-CSS characterization suite). Those now pass because `browseRoute`/`searchRoute` are defined in `test-helpers.ts`, which the task EXPLICITLY requires to be extracted ('browseRoute, searchRoute ... must be extracted into a shared helper module'). This is the minimal, in-scope way to satisfy both 'PRESERVE EVERY EXISTING TEST SCENARIO' and 'npm test passes'. (3) `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0; `test-helpers.ts` is correctly excluded from the production build (verified: `wwwroot/dist/app/` contains no test-helpers.js and no .test.js files). DESCRIBE-BLOCK PLACEMENT: the task's per-file breakdown names 5 describe blocks, but the monolith actually contains 10 top-level blocks. The implementer correctly preserved ALL of them (dropping any would be a CRITICAL violation) by placing the extra blocks in the most thematically-related files: 'import characterization' + 'dead CSS selector characterization' → start-app.test.ts (both pin app.ts's module surface/retained imports/DOM output); 'render output depends only on result' + 'rendering lifecycle' → render-orchestration.test.ts (render coordination/lifecycle). Each file's JSDoc header documents its full contents and the grouping rationale. HELPER EXTRACTION: `src/app/test-helpers.ts` exports every required name (fileEntry, browseResult, joinPathHelper, browseRoute, searchRoute, flush, SetupCtx, setup, setupCleared, dataRows, rowByName, cellsOf, clickNameLink, buttonsByText, fetchMock, installAppTestLifecycle). `mockResponse` is correctly IMPORTED from the existing `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts` (task-27) rather than re-defined. tsconfig.json adds `src/app/test-helpers.ts` to `exclude`, mirroring the `src/test-utils/**` pattern. CLEANLINESS: I checked every import in all 5 files — zero unused imports (including the multi-name single-line vitest/router/format imports). No dead code, no debug leftovers, no duplicated logic, no magic literals (ISO/ISO_FMT are named constants). `installAppTestLifecycle()` is called exactly once per file (vitest scopes hooks per-file, so this is correct). The `export let fetchMock` live-binding mechanism (rebound in beforeEach) is sound and documented; all fetchMock.mock.calls assertions pass. The original `src/app.test.ts` is deleted. ORGANIZATION: each file has a single responsibility, a thorough JSDoc header carrying over the monolith's contract decisions, and consistent section dividers matching the original style. No issues found.

workflowData.research:
## Synthesized Research Summary

### Files Examined
- **Backend production**: Controllers/FilesController.cs, Services/FileService.cs, FileServiceOptions.cs, Program.cs, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json
- **Frontend production**: src/app.ts, src/app.css, src/router.ts, src/format.ts, src/api.ts
- **Backend tests**: FileServiceTests.cs, IFileServiceContractTests.cs, FileSystemModelsTests.cs, FilesControllerTests.cs, FilesControllerContractTests.cs, FileServiceOptionsTests.cs, ProgramWiringTests.cs
- **Frontend tests**: src/app.test.ts, src/api.test.ts, src/router.test.ts, src/router.dom.test.ts, src/format.test.ts, src/readme.deep-linking.test.ts, vitest.config.ts

### Key Findings

**Dead Code (production)**: 
- Backend: Unused `_logger` field in FilesController; unused `using` directives
- Frontend: 3 unused imports in app.ts (parseHash, parentPath, basename); 3 dead CSS classes (`.folder-row`, `.separator`, `.upload-btn`)

**Dead / Tautological Tests (~76 tests, ~900 lines)**:
- FileSystemModelsTests.cs (28 tests) — tests compiler-guaranteed record features
- FileServiceOptionsTests.cs (6 tests) — tests trivial auto-property behavior
- IFileServiceContractTests.cs (12 tests) — reflection-based interface signature checks (compiler-enforced)
- FilesControllerContractTests.cs (~30 tests) — reflection-based controller attribute/signature checks (compiler-enforced)
- Frontend: "shared response types" block in api.test.ts (3 tests); readme.deep-linking.test.ts (tests README prose, not code)
- ProgramWiringTests template-preservation tests guard extinct scaffolding

**Monolith Files**:
- `src/app.ts` (472 lines) — mixes DOM construction, route dispatch, toolbar handlers, browse/search rendering, DOM builder helpers → split into domain modules
- `src/app.test.ts` (940 lines) — covers 6 distinct concerns → split by domain
- `TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs` (~558 lines) — embeds FakeFileService (80+ lines) inline → extract

**Duplicate Code**:
- Backend: Identical 5-catch blocks duplicated across 6 controller endpoints (~210 lines); extract to helper/middleware
- Backend tests: `CreateFormFile` helper duplicated in FileServiceTests and FilesControllerTests; CaughtExceptions pattern repeated 7×
- Frontend tests: `mockResponse` helper duplicated in api.test.ts and app.test.ts

**Bugs**:
- Backend: Null-forgiving operator on `Path.GetDirectoryName()` can crash on root paths; TOCTOU race in Delete (check-then-act); unnormalized response paths from raw query parameter
- Frontend: Memory leak from discarded `subscribe()` unsubscribe handle (hashchange listeners accumulate); race condition in `render()` with no deduplication/abort; unhandled promise rejections in action button handlers; missing error handling in upload loop

**Performance**:
- `FileExtensionContentTypeProvider` instantiated per request → cache static
- `Directory.GetDirectories` + `Directory.GetFiles` walk directory twice → single pass

**Code Smells**:
- `FileService` constructor creates home directory during DI resolution (startup failure risk); no null-check on `IFormFile`; recursive `CopyDirectory` with no depth limit; `ComputeParent` uses manual string ops instead of `Path.GetDirectoryName`
- Frontend: `void route;` workaround for unused parameters; module-level DOM refs create stale-closure race; fragile `leafByText` test helper
- `FileServiceOptions` lives in root namespace (inconsistent)

**Magic Values**:
- `MaxSearchResults = 500` hardcoded; 1024 inline in formatBytes; ISO_FMT expected string in tests stales independently; 550 magic buffer count in search-cap test

### Summary
The codebase is moderate-sized (frontend SPA + backend .NET Web API). All 10 improvement categories have identified issues. The most impactful changes are: (1) deleting ~900 lines of tautological/contract tests, (2) splitting monolith files (app.ts, app.test.ts, FilesControllerTests.cs), (3) fixing the hashchange listener memory leak and render race condition in the frontend, (4) refactoring the duplicated catch-block pattern in the controller, and (5) addressing the TOCTOU race and null-handling bugs in FileService.

tasks.title: Fix frontend rendering lifecycle (hashchange listener leak + render race)
tasks.prompt:
In `src/app.ts`, two related lifecycle bugs in the rendering system must be fixed together (they share the startApp/render code path):

(a) MEMORY LEAK — `startApp` calls `subscribe(render)` and DISCARDS the returned unsubscribe function. Every call to `startApp` (e.g., across re-mounts, hot reloads, or repeated test setup) accumulates a new `hashchange` listener on `window` that is never removed. The bug is documented in a code comment in `src/app.test.ts`. Fix: introduce a module-level variable `let unsubscribe: (() => void) | null = null;` alongside the existing module-level DOM refs (`resultsEl`, etc.). At the top of `startApp`, if `unsubscribe` is non-null, call it (to remove the previous listener) and set it back to null. Then assign `unsubscribe = subscribe(render);`. This guarantees at most one active listener per mount.

(b) RENDER RACE — `render()` is async. If the user navigates twice in quick succession (e.g., clicks a folder then clicks a sub-folder before the first fetch resolves), two `render()` calls race and the slower one's stale data can overwrite the faster one's fresh data in `resultsEl`. Fix: introduce a module-level monotonic counter `let renderToken = 0;`. At the start of `render()`, increment the counter and capture `const myToken = ++renderToken;`. After EACH `await` inside `render()`, check `if (myToken !== renderToken) return;` — if a newer render has started, abort WITHOUT touching `resultsEl`, `breadcrumbEl`, or `statusEl`. Only the most-recently-started render gets to commit its data (i.e., reach the `renderBrowse(...)` / `renderSearch(...)` calls).

These changes are interdependent (both touch startApp/render state) so they belong in one task. Preserve all currently-observable behavior — the existing `src/app.test.ts` tests must still pass. In particular, the existing comment in app.test.ts's afterEach notes that hashchange listeners accumulate and so the test resets the hash before unstubbing fetch; that comment may become outdated after this fix and should be updated (by the test-writing phase). Verification: `npm test` passes; the test-writing phase may add new tests for (i) multiple `startApp` calls do not multiply hashchange listeners, and (ii) a slower fetch that resolves after a newer navigation is discarded (its data does not appear in `.results`).

The handler may now be async without leaking a rejection. Do NOT change the success-path behavior — when the handler resolves normally, no error message is shown. (Note: the status-footer error display is consistent with how `render()` already surfaces fetch errors, so this gives the user a single, consistent error surface.) tasks.title: Handle per-file errors in the upload loop

Fix: wrap each per-file `api.upload` call in its own try/catch (or use `Promise.allSettled` if you convert to parallel — but sequential is fine and preserves ordering). If all succeeded, leave the success-path status alone (the re-render will refresh it via renderBrowse/renderSearch). Do NOT change the upload endpoint, the input clearing, or the re-render on full success. tasks.title: Extract the duplicated 5-catch block into a controller helper
tasks.prompt:
In `Controllers/FilesController.cs`, each of the seven endpoints (Browse, Search, Upload, Download, Delete, Move, Copy) wraps its work in an IDENTICAL five-catch block (`ArgumentException`, `UnauthorizedAccessException`, `DirectoryNotFoundException`, `FileNotFoundException`, `IOException` — each translated to `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })`). That is roughly 210 lines of duplicated boilerplate. Extract a private helper that wraps a delegate and performs the translation, so each endpoint becomes a one-line delegation:

  - `private IActionResult Execute(Func<IActionResult> body)` — synchronous endpoints. Body: `try { return body(); } catch (ArgumentException ex) { return BadRequest(...); } catch (UnauthorizedAccessException ex) { ... } catch (DirectoryNotFoundException ex) { ... } catch (FileNotFoundException ex) { ... } catch (IOException ex) { ... }`
  - `private async Task<IActionResult> ExecuteAsync(Func<Task<IActionResult>> body)` — for the Upload endpoint (which must await the service call). Same catch list.

Then refactor each endpoint to delegate:
  - Browse → `Execute(() => Ok(_service.Browse(path ?? string.Empty)))`
  - Search → `Execute(() => Ok(_service.Search(query ?? string.Empty, path ?? string.Empty)))`
  - Upload → `ExecuteAsync(async () => { await _service.UploadAsync(path ?? string.Empty, file); return Ok(new { path = /* existing normalized-path logic from task-15 */ }); })`
  - Download → `Execute(() => { var full = _service.ResolveFullPath(path); var contentType = ContentTypeProvider.TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream"; return PhysicalFile(full, contentType, Path.GetFileName(full)); })`
  - Delete → `Execute(() => { _service.Delete(path); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`
  - Move → `Execute(() => { _service.Move(request); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`
  - Copy → `Execute(() => { _service.Copy(request); return Ok(new { success = true }); })`

PRESERVE EVERY OBSERVABLE BEHAVIOR — the response status codes (200/400), the `error`/`success`/`path` payload shapes, the exact exception-to-BadRequest mapping, and especially the 'non-translated exceptions propagate' guarantee covered by `Controller_DoesNotSwallowUnhandledExceptions` in FilesControllerTests.cs. Keep the XML doc comments on each endpoint method. Verification: `dotnet build TestProject.sln`; `dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` — all FilesControllerTests pass without modification.

tasks.title: Deduplicate the mockResponse test helper into a shared module
tasks.prompt: The `mockResponse(opts)` helper (a minimal fetch `Response` stand-in: takes `{ ok?, status?, body?, text? }`, returns a `Response`-shaped object with synchronous `json()`/`text()` accessors) is duplicated in `src/api.test.ts` and `src/app.test.ts` with effectively identical semantics. Extract it into a shared test utility module `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts` that exports the `mockResponse` function and (if convenient) a `MockResponseOptions` type/interface describing the opts shape. Replace both inline copies with imports from the new module. Do NOT change the function semantics (the api.test.ts and app.test.ts versions differ very slightly in their `json()` fallback for the `text`-only case — pick the union of both behaviors so all existing call sites still work, or inspect both and choose the more permissive implementation). Verify the test-utils folder is excluded from the production `tsc` build (it should be, since `tsconfig.json` excludes `src/**/*.test.ts` — but `src/test-utils/*.ts` is NOT a test file, so it WOULD be included in the build. To avoid shipping test utilities in the production bundle, either name the file `src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts`-adjacent via a separate tsconfig, OR simpler: place the helper at `src/__test-utils__/mock-response.ts` and add `__test-utils__` to the tsconfig `exclude` array, OR place it inline-as `mock-response.ts` next to the test files and add `src/test-utils/**` to the `exclude` array in `tsconfig.json`). tasks.title: Split app.ts monolith into focused domain modules
tasks.prompt:
`src/app.ts` is a ~470-line monolith that mixes DOM construction, route dispatch, toolbar handlers, browse rendering, search rendering, and DOM builder helpers — six distinct responsibilities. Split it into focused modules under a new `src/app/` directory, each with a single responsibility:

  - `src/app/dom-builders.ts` — exports `buildTable`, `makeNavLink`, `makeParentRow`, `makeBrowseRow`, `makeActionButton`, `makeSearchRow`, `renderBreadcrumb`. These are pure DOM-construction helpers; they need imports of `api` (for download URLs in makeBrowseRow/makeSearchRow), `formatBytes`/`formatDate` (for cell content), and `toBrowseHash`/`navigate` (for nav links). They also need the action-button handler wiring (which now calls back into the render orchestrator — see below) so accept an `onRerender` callback or import the orchestrator lazily.
  - `src/app/render-browse.ts` — exports `renderBrowse`. Operates against the shared DOM context (passed in or via a context module — see below).
  - `src/app/render-search.ts` — exports `renderSearch`.
  - `src/app/toolbar-handlers.ts` — exports `doSearch`, `handleUpload`.
  - `src/app/render-orchestrator.ts` — exports `render` (the route dispatcher), owns the module-level DOM refs (`resultsEl`, `statusEl`, `breadcrumbEl`, `searchInput`, `uploadInput`), the render token (task-20), the unsubscribe handle (task-20), and an `init(domRefs)` or similar that startApp calls to bind the refs.
  - `src/app.ts` (slim entry) — exports `startApp` (constructs the DOM via dom-builders, wires events, binds refs into the orchestrator, kicks off the initial render) and the bootstrap guard (`const appRoot = document.getElementById('app'); if (appRoot) startApp(appRoot);`). RE-EXPORT `renderBrowse` and `renderSearch` from `./app/render-browse.js` and `./app/render-search.js` so `src/app.test.ts`'s existing `import { startApp, renderBrowse, renderSearch } from './app'` continues to resolve without modification.

A clean pattern for the shared DOM context: define a small `src/app/context.ts` (or put it in render-orchestrator.ts) that holds the refs and exposes getters/setters. dom-builders and the render functions read from this context. This replaces the current pattern of bare module-level `let resultsEl!: HTMLElement` variables that live in app.ts.

PRESERVE ALL OBSERVABLE BEHAVIOR — the public API of `app.ts` is unchanged (same exports, same signatures, same DOM output, same event handling, same navigation, same error surfaces). This is a PURELY STRUCTURAL refactor; the test-writing phase should produce characterization tests first (pinning current behavior) so the change is provably behavior-preserving. Verify: `npm run build` succeeds; `npm test` passes (all existing `src/app.test.ts` scenarios still pass without modification).

tasks.title: Split app.test.ts monolith into per-domain test files
tasks.prompt:
`src/app.test.ts` is a ~940-line monolith covering six distinct concerns in one file. Split it into focused test files matching the production-module decomposition from task-28:

  - `src/app/start-app.test.ts` — the 'app module surface' and 'startApp' describe blocks (module exports, DOM structure, dialog open/close wiring, shared-widget embedded-vs-dialog behavior).
  - `src/app/render-browse.test.ts` — the entire `renderBrowse` describe block (table headers, rows, columns, navigation, actions column, breadcrumb, status footer).
  - `src/app/render-search.test.ts` — the entire `renderSearch` describe block (table headers, rows, navigation, status).
  - `src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts` — the 'render orchestration' describe block (initial render on startApp, dispatch after navigation, error display, clear-previous-results).
  - `src/app/toolbar-handlers.test.ts` — the 'toolbar handlers' describe block (search button, Enter key, upload happy path and empty-files case).

The shared test helpers (`mockResponse`, `fileEntry`, `browseResult`, `joinPathHelper`, `browseRoute`, `searchRoute`, `flush`, `SetupCtx`, `setup`, `setupCleared`, `dataRows`, `rowByName`, `cellsOf`, `clickNameLink`, `buttonsByText`, and the test lifecycle `beforeEach`/`afterEach` that stubs `fetch`) must be extracted into a shared helper module such as `src/app/test-helpers.ts` (and excluded from the production build via the tsconfig `exclude` array, similar to task-27). Each split test file imports what it needs. If `mockResponse` was already extracted to `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts` by task-27, import from there instead of re-defining. PRESERVE EVERY EXISTING TEST SCENARIO — do not drop, weaken, or merge assertions. The total test count after the split must equal the total before (minus anything task-7 already removed). Verify: `npm test` passes; the distributed test files together cover the same surface as the original monolith.

plan.strategy: Address all ten improvement categories surfaced by the research in five dependency-safe phases. PHASE 1 (task-1 through task-7) deletes ~900 lines of tautological and dead tests — reflection-based contract tests, record-shape tests, README-prose tests, and compiler-enforced type assertions. These are mechanical, self-contained wins that all run in parallel with no dependencies. PHASE 2 (task-8 through task-19) applies production code changes: mechanical cleanups (unused logger field, dead imports, dead CSS), perf wins (static FileExtensionContentTypeProvider, single-pass DirectoryInfo enumeration), bug fixes (Move null-forgiving, Delete TOCTOU, normalized upload response, depth-limited CopyDirectory, ComputeParent via Path.GetDirectoryName), a structural code smell (lazy home-directory creation to decouple DI resolution from disk I/O), and a namespace consistency fix (FileServiceOptions -> TestProject.Configuration). The FileService.cs changes are sequenced so the lazy-init refactor (task-16) lands before the per-method changes (task-12, 13, 14, 17, 18) that depend on its `Root` accessor; the FilesController.cs changes are sequenced so the small fixes (task-8 logger, task-11 cache, task-15 normalize) land before the catch-block extraction (task-24). PHASE 3 (task-20 through task-23) fixes the frontend rendering lifecycle bugs (hashchange listener leak + render race, fixed together as one coherent change), plus error handling in action button handlers and the upload loop, plus the unused-`route`-parameter cleanup (which must follow the render-lifecycle fix since both edit render()). PHASE 4 (task-24 through task-27) refactors duplicate code: the controller catch-block extraction, FakeFileService extraction, shared CreateFormFile helper, and deduplicated mockResponse helper. PHASE 5 (task-28 and task-29) splits the two monolith files (app.ts into six focused domain modules with preserved public-API re-exports; app.test.ts into per-domain test files with shared helpers extracted). Within each phase tasks are parallelizable; cross-phase and same-file dependencies are explicit in each task's `dependencies` array. All refactors preserve observable behavior — characterization tests are produced by the test-writing phase before any structural change, and each task's verification step runs `dotnet build`, `dotnet test`, `npm run build`, or `npm test` as appropriate. The plan keeps the codebase interesting (the split surfaces a clean domain-module structure; the lifecycle fix is a real concurrency correction; the lazy-init decoupling is a meaningful DI improvement) while remaining strictly true to task.md (the end-to-end file-browsing flow, deep-linkable SPA, and the bonus dialog/delete/move/copy features all continue to work).
